_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to prove and apply angle relationships involving radii, diameters, chords and tangents of circles.

_source.w: We are learning to prove and apply angle relationships involving radii, diameters, chords and tangents of circles.
y: Circle theorems represent some of the most elegant results in geometry, connecting seemingly different properties through logical proof.
Proving these relationships develops deductive reasoning — the ability to build logical arguments from established facts.
Circle geometry is applied in engineering, architecture, optics and orbital mechanics.
eg: Proving that the angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc.
Applying the theorem: 'The angle in a semicircle is a right angle (Thales' theorem).'
Proving that a tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency.
Finding missing angles using the alternate segment theorem.
Proving that opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary.
Applying circle theorems to solve multi-step geometric problems.
